text.title: Python Ecosystem Practices Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the practices-python-ecosystem methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: pyproject.toml (PEP 621) MUST be the single source of project metadata. setup.py / setup.cfg are forbidden in new projects.
rule.rationale: PEP 621 unifies metadata across build backends; mixed setup.py + pyproject leads to drift and confused tooling.

rule.statement: Project MUST use the src/<package>/ layout. Top-level package directories next to tests/ are forbidden.
rule.rationale: src/ layout prevents accidental import-from-cwd that hides install bugs; pytest + editable install behave consistently.

rule.statement: Linting + formatting MUST go through ruff (ruff check + ruff format). black, isort, flake8 are forbidden in the same project.
rule.rationale: ruff is one binary replacing the trio; running all three doubles config and slows pre-commit.

rule.statement: Type checking MUST run mypy with strict=true (or matching ruff/pyright preset). disallow_untyped_defs and no_implicit_optional are required.
rule.rationale: Strict mode catches the bugs casual typing misses; libraries shipping types MUST validate strictly to honour the py.typed contract.

rule.statement: print() MUST NOT appear in production code paths (T20 in ruff). Use logging.* instead.
rule.rationale: print bypasses log config, breaks structured logging, and floods stdout in production.

rule.statement: Dependencies MUST be locked via uv.lock or requirements.txt generated by pip-compile. Floating versions in production deploys are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a lock, the build version drifts on every install; reproducibility breaks.
